rubricFactors.factor: No naloxone access in past 30 days

aiInsights: OD frequency accelerating: 10-month gap OD1→OD2, 5.5 months OD2→OD3, 4.5 months OD3→OD4
Post-incarceration release pattern: 2 of 4 ODs occurred within 2 weeks of CCDC release
Fentanyl + benzos significantly reduces naloxone effectiveness — multiple doses required
Hep C progression without treatment increases medical complication risk at 12-month horizon
MAT discontinuation pattern (2 starts, 2 stops) indicates systemic engagement barriers

redFlags.flag: Opioid + Benzodiazepine combination (5x increased OD risk per CDC)
redFlags.action: Immediate naloxone kit + benzo tapering plan
ED DART protocol + warm handoff to MAT

redFlags.flag: Post-incarceration release + no MAT bridge from CCDC

rubricFactors.factor: No naloxone access (OD without own kit)

aiInsights: Pregnant patient with active fentanyl + meth use — dual risk profile for mother and fetus
Neonatal Opioid Withdrawal Syndrome (NOWS) risk: high — requires Subutex (buprenorphine mono) not Suboxone
MAT discontinuation within 6 weeks of initiation — indicates need for intensive outreach support
Prenatal depression score (Edinburgh 18) suggests high psychiatric co-morbidity burden
Housing instability during pregnancy compounds risk of APS involvement post-delivery

redFlags.flag: OD #3 while 14 weeks pregnant — fetal monitoring required
Fentanyl + meth — high risk of NOWS and preterm labor
Unstable housing during pregnancy — APS notification may be required

aiInsights: 5 months continuous Vivitrol — treatment retention is the strongest predictor of sustained recovery
PTSD improving (PCL-5: 52→34) — co-occurring mental health treatment is reducing SUD relapse risk
Chronic pain remains a risk factor — non-opioid pain management plan should be formalized
Family engagement and return to employment are strong protective factors
Veteran status — VA coordination ensures continuity of care

redFlags.flag: Chronic pain without opioid-free pain plan formally documented
redFlags.action: Consult with pain management for non-opioid protocol

redFlags.flag: PTSD still moderate — continued therapy engagement critical
redFlags.action: Maintain monthly PTSD counseling at Daymark

rubricFactors.factor: No naloxone access in past 30 days

aiInsights: 5 ODs — highest OD burden in active Catawba County caseload
Xylazine co-use makes standard naloxone reversal unreliable — wound care also needed
Chronically unhoused — encampment-based outreach is primary engagement pathway
COPD compounds respiratory depression risk during OD events
No MAT history suggests severe engagement barriers — low-barrier options needed (mobile dosing)

redFlags.flag: Xylazine (tranq) detected — standard Narcan may not fully reverse
redFlags.action: Wound care check + extended observation at CVMC

redFlags.flag: 5th OD — chronic high-risk unhoused patient with COPD

aiInsights: Untreated schizophrenia severely limits treatment engagement — psychiatric stabilization prerequisite for MAT
Poly-substance (tri-substance) pattern is highest-risk profile in Catawba active caseload
Unhoused + SMI = extremely high barrier to standard outpatient treatment pathways
Language barrier (Spanish-speaking) requires bilingual outreach and interpreter services

redFlags.flag: No insurance — barriers to MAT initiation

rubricFactors.factor: Fentanyl only — high potency, low tolerance
Young adult (21) — early intervention high ROI

aiInsights: Young adult (21) with 2 ODs in 6 weeks — rapid escalation pattern
Parents' home is a protective factor but may also enable access to substances
No prior MAT — high potential for MAT initiation as first treatment episode
Family engagement critical — parents should be enrolled in naloxone training

redFlags.flag: OD #2 at age 21 — 6 weeks after first OD, no treatment
No prior SUD treatment — needs warm handoff at CVMC

rubricFactors.factor: No OD in last 30 days (last: Jan 2026)
Declined MAT at prior contact — barrier

aiInsights: Post-incarceration window: 23 days since release — still in highest-risk period
No MAT bridge at CCDC release is a systemic gap — county SLA requires outreach within 48h of release
Meth + fentanyl combination is increasingly common and associated with higher lethality
Declined MAT at outreach — motivational interviewing approach recommended

redFlags.flag: Post-incarceration, no MAT bridge, meth + fentanyl
